context: This eval tests the body / template compliance rubric's ability to distinguish 'all required template fields are physically present' from 'a filled required answer is made unreliable by another statement in the same body'. The synthetic PR template has a Compatibility / Migration section with explicit Yes/No fields. The draft body fills that section as backward compatible with no migration needed, but Summary much earlier in the same body says replacing `messageBridge.rawCaption` with `messageBridge.safeCaption` is a breaking change for plugins importing the old helper. The tile-prescribed result is `Slight deviation`: do not ask for sections already present and do not demand a full rewrite, but do draft a concise clarification comment asking the author which compatibility statement is correct and whether migration guidance is needed. `Matches well enough` with `No comment needed` is wrong because the inconsistency makes a required answer unreliable.

checklist.name: Recognizes the Compatibility / Migration fields are present but unreliable
checklist.description: The agent notices the body includes `Backward compatible? (Yes/No) Yes`, `Config/env changes? (Yes/No) No`, `Migration needed? (Yes/No) No`, and upgrade steps. It does not incorrectly report the Compatibility / Migration section as missing, but it also does not treat those answers as safely complete once another body sentence contradicts them.

checklist.name: Finds the distant breaking-change contradiction
checklist.description: The agent identifies that Summary says replacing `messageBridge.rawCaption` with `messageBridge.safeCaption` is a breaking change for plugins importing the old helper, while Compatibility / Migration says the PR is backward compatible and no migration is needed. It should connect these distant statements even though they use different field wording.

checklist.name: Classifies as Slight deviation with author clarification, not no-comment match
checklist.description: The result bucket uses the exact label `Result: Slight deviation` because the template is structurally filled but a required answer set is unreliable due to a body-local contradiction. `Matches well enough` with `No comment needed` is incorrect because the author needs to clarify the compatibility claim. `Significant deviation` is also incorrect because this is one focused clarification, not a broad template rewrite.

checklist.name: Drafts a concise clarification comment
checklist.description: The suggested comment asks the PR author to clarify the conflict between the breaking-change summary and the Compatibility / Migration answers, including whether migration or upgrade guidance is needed. It must not be `No comment needed`, and it must not leave the contradiction only as a private/manual triager note.

checklist.name: Does not over-ask for already-present template content
checklist.description: The agent does not ask for Summary, Security Impact, Compatibility / Migration, Repro / Verification, User-visible changes, Environment, Human verification, or the Yes/No field to be added, because those fields are already present in the same body. It asks only for clarification/correction of the inconsistent compatibility claim rather than requesting a full template rewrite.

checklist.name: Explains why author clarification is needed
checklist.description: The output briefly explains that the body-local template content is present, but the body gives incompatible answers about breaking compatibility and migration. It makes clear the author should resolve which statement is correct before submission.
